title: Reading Files
description: Open and read data from files.

slides.title: Working with Files 📁
slides.content:
# Why File Handling?

Programs need to:
* 📖 Read configuration
* 💾 Save user data
* 📝 Process text files
* 📊 Handle CSV/JSON

**File handling** lets you persist data!

slides.title: The open() Function 🔓
slides.content:
# Opening Files

```python
file = open("filename.txt", "r")  # Read mode
content = file.read()
file.close()  # Always close!
```

**Modes:**
* `"r"` — Read (default)
* `"w"` — Write (overwrites)
* `"a"` — Append
* `"r+"` — Read and write

slides.title: Reading a File 💻
slides.codeSnippet:
# Simulate file content (in real usage, read from actual file)
file_content = """Hello, World!
This is line 2.
This is line 3."""

# Simulating reading
lines = file_content.split("\n")
print("All content:")
print(file_content)
print("\nLine by line:")
for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
    print(f"Line {i}: {line}")

quizData.question: What mode opens a file for reading?
quizData.explanation: "r" is read mode — the default mode for open().

slides.title: The with Statement ✨
slides.content:
# Better: Context Manager

```python
# Automatically closes file!
with open("file.txt", "r") as file:
    content = file.read()
# File closed here automatically
```

`with` ensures the file is closed even if errors occur!

slides.title: Reading Methods 📖
slides.content:
# Different Ways to Read

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `read()` | Entire file as string |
| `readline()` | One line at a time |
| `readlines()` | List of all lines |
| `for line in file:` | Iterate lines |

slides.title: Reading Methods 💻
slides.codeSnippet:
# Simulating file reading methods
content = "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3\n"
lines = content.strip().split("\n")

print("read() returns everything:")
print(repr(content))

print("\nreadlines() returns a list:")
print(lines)

print("\nIterate line by line:")
for line in lines:
    print(f">> {line}")

quizData.question: Which method reads the entire file at once?
quizData.explanation: read() reads the entire file content as one string.

slides.title: Common Errors ⚠️
slides.content:
## File Handling Pitfalls

```python
# FileNotFoundError
open("doesnt_exist.txt")  # ❌

# Always use with statement
file = open("data.txt")
# forgot file.close()  # ❌
```

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Memory-Efficient Reading

For large files, read line by line:

```python
with open("big_file.txt") as f:
    for line in f:  # One line at a time
        process(line)
```

Don't load huge files into memory!

quizData.question: Why use 'with open()' instead of just 'open()'?
quizData.explanation: 'with' automatically closes the file, even if errors occur!
